subject: SCIENCE
title: What Living Things Need
introduction: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “What Living Things Need”.
A key word used in the lesson “What Living Things Need”.
A key word used in the lesson “What Living Things Need”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: All organisms need shelter in same form

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

options: Plants make sugars using light energy.

feedback.correct: Correct: Plants make sugars using light energy.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. Plants eat soil as food. and Animals make food from sunlight. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “What Living Things Need”?

options.text: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.
All organisms need shelter in same form

items.explanation: The correct response is “Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

options.text: All organisms need shelter in same form

items.explanation: The correct response is “plant—light and water” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Plants eat soil as food” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

options.text: Plants make sugars using light energy.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Plants make sugars using light energy.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “both need suitable space” because it follows the lesson explanation: Living things need resources, but not in identical ways. Plants make sugars and need light, water, gases, and nutrients; animals obtain food.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for What Living Things Need; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in What Living Things Need.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
